perturbation.original_text: B. The charges for the services set forth in this Agreement shall be invoiced monthly. ABW agrees to pay said charges within thirty (30) days of the monthly invoice date. ABW may issue a purchase order for billing purposes. The invoices must reference that purchase order number and be sent to the "Bill To" address stated on the purchase order. The terms of this negotiated Agreement shall supercede those contained on that purchase order.
perturbation.changed_text: B. The charges for the services set forth in this Agreement shall be invoiced monthly. ABW agrees to pay said charges within fifteen (15) days of the monthly invoice date. ABW may issue a purchase order for billing purposes. The invoices must reference that purchase order number and be sent to the "Bill To" address stated on the purchase order. The terms of this negotiated Agreement shall supercede those contained on that purchase order.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment terms in Section 3.B to require payment within fifteen (15) days of the invoice date, while Section 3.H states that PCQ may terminate the agreement if an invoice is not paid within forty-five (45) days after receipt. This creates ambiguity regarding the actual payment deadline and when PCQ can terminate the agreement for non-payment. The perturbation is located in Section 3.B, while the contradiction is directly related to Section 3.H.
perturbation.contradicted_text: H. In the event that any invoice is not paid by ABW within forty-five (45) days after receipt, and when no discrepancy issues have been identified by ABW which are in some stage of resolution, after giving notice to ABW, PCQ may terminate this agreement and ABW's access to and use of SOFTWARE and HYPERFEED provided hereunder unless ABW pays such invoice prior to the termination date Source: PCQUOTE COM INC, S-1/A, 7/21/1999 specified in the Termination Notice. The remedies contained herein are cumulative and are in addition to all other rights and remedies available to PCQ under this Agreement, by operation of law, or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms in Section 3.B to require payment within fifteen (15) days of the invoice date, while Section 3.H states that PCQ may terminate the agreement if an invoice is not paid within forty-five (45) days after receipt. This creates a clear contradiction regarding the actual payment deadline, causing ambiguity and potential disputes over when PCQ can terminate the agreement for non-payment. The original text allowed for 30 days, which, while different than 45 days allowed for termination, could be interpreted as compatible, but the modified version is clearly an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: D. Any payments which have not been received by PCQ within thirty (30) days of the Invoice date shall be subject to a FINANCE CHARGE of 1.0% per month which is a corresponding ANNUAL PERCENTAGE RATE of 12% on the outstanding balance.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Any payments which have not been received by PCQ within forty-five (45) days of the Invoice date shall be subject to a FINANCE CHARGE of 1.0% per month which is a corresponding ANNUAL PERCENTAGE RATE of 12% on the outstanding balance.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 3.B now stipulates that invoices must be paid within fifteen (15) days, while Section 3.D states that a finance charge applies to payments not received within forty-five (45) days. This contradicts the payment deadline set in Section 3.B and creates confusion regarding when late fees are applicable. This contradiction affects the enforceability of the payment terms. The perturbation is made at Section 3.D and it contradicts Section 3.B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B. The charges for the services set forth in this Agreement shall be invoiced monthly. ABW agrees to pay said charges within fifteen (15) days of the monthly invoice date. ABW may issue a purchase order for billing purposes. The invoices must reference that purchase order number and be sent to the "Bill To" address stated on the purchase order. The terms of this negotiated Agreement shall supercede those contained on that purchase order.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 3.D changes the payment deadline for invoices from thirty (30) days to forty-five (45) days. This directly contradicts Section 3.B, which states that ABW agrees to pay charges within fifteen (15) days of the invoice date. The two sections provide conflicting deadlines for payment, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined in the instructions under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: I. Upon termination as provided for in this Agreement, ABW will pay all charges for services and fees for the entire month in which that termination becomes effective.
perturbation.changed_text: I. Upon termination as provided for in this Agreement, ABW will pay all charges for services and fees only up to the date of termination in the month in which that termination becomes effective.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment obligations upon termination. Initially, ABW was responsible for paying for the entire month of termination. Now, ABW is only liable for charges up to the termination date within that month. This contradicts the original term in Section 3.I and creates uncertainty about the extent of payment obligation upon termination. Since the change has been made at section 3.I it contradcts the original definition of section 3.I
perturbation.contradicted_text: I. Upon termination as provided for in this Agreement, ABW will pay all charges for services and fees only up to the date of termination in the month in which that termination becomes effective.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated ABW would pay for the entire month upon termination, while the changed text states ABW will only pay up to the date of termination. This creates a direct conflict and contradiction regarding the payment obligation, thus a strong in-text contradiction exists as it directly alters a previously defined obligation. The contradiction is within section I of the agreement. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, reinforcing the change to the original statement in Section 3.I
